name: github-hosted-pypi
cost: Free / open-source (MIT). Runs on free GitHub Pages + GitHub Actions; no server cost.
language: HTML/CSS/JavaScript (GitHub Pages static site) + Python (GitHub Actions automation scripts)
last_updated: 2025-01-24 (latest release v0.3.2; last code push 2025-02-10; repo metadata touched 2026-05-15)
perf_notes: No published benchmarks. The index is static HTML served by GitHub Pages' global CDN, so read latency/throughput is GitHub's, not the project's. Install cost is dominated by pip cloning the whole referenced Git repository at a tag and building from source (VCS install), which is materially slower than downloading a prebuilt wheel; there is no artifact caching.
scale_notes: No self-managed nodes — availability/scaling are inherited from GitHub Pages (CDN-backed, effectively HA for reads, stateless). Practical limits are GitHub Pages soft limits (~1 GB site size, ~100 GB/month bandwidth, ~10 builds/hour) and the practicality of maintaining many packages as static HTML committed via PRs. No HA/clustering, sharding, or multi-node story of its own.
summary: A fork-it-yourself template that turns a GitHub Pages repo into a public PEP 503-shaped "simple" index whose entries are git+https VCS links to (optionally private) GitHub repos, giving you a pip-installable private package index with no server, no database, and no artifact hosting.
extras: Index is a static PEP 503-shaped site on GitHub Pages: no server, no database, no artifact storage — per-package anchors are VCS links of the form git+https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>@<tag>#egg=<name>-<version> that pip clones and builds from source (verified on the live demo: <a href="git+https://github.com/astariul/public-hello@0.2#egg=public-hello-0.2">)

PEP 503 is only partial: the page structure is simple-index-shaped, but the per-file hrefs are git+https VCS links with #egg= fragments and no hash, not the file-download URLs PEP 503 mandates — so it works only with pip's legacy egg-fragment/VCS handling and is pip-only in practice, not portable across installers

Client split is real and verified, not a generic install-clients-move-together case: pip installs via --extra-index-url, but spec-conformant clients that expect file links + a sha256/md5 hash reject the egg-fragment VCS anchors — uv fails outright (astral-sh/uv#2602: uv treats the #egg= fragment as a malformed hash), and Poetry/PDM/Hatch/Pipenv likewise do not consume egg-fragment VCS index links; twine has no upload target (static site, no upload API) and flit has no install capability

Installed exactly like a real index: pip install <pkg> --extra-index-url https://<owner>.github.io/<repo>/ — pip queries the static index and resolves the per-package page to its git+https VCS link (no manual copy-paste)

Packages are registered/updated/deleted through manually-triggered GitHub Actions workflows that open a PR; nothing is published until the PR is merged (PR acts as a review/staging gate, and Git/PR history is the change audit trail)

Privacy is delegated to GitHub: the index and its package names/versions are public, but linked code lives in private GitHub repos that pip can only clone with GitHub credentials (.netrc login/PAT), e.g. in Docker via a mounted secret

Dependency-confusion awareness (not enforcement): each package page queries the public PyPI JSON API client-side and, if a same-named package with a higher version exists on PyPI, replaces the shown install command with a warning
Web UI with per-package cards, version list, author/homepage links, and README markdown rendered in-browser via marked.js
Distributed as a GitHub template repository — you click 'Use this template' to create your own index; uses GitHub Pages' built-in HTTPS for TLS with no TLS configuration of its own
Latest release v0.3.2 (2025-01-24) only fixes SemVer comparison (#83); no feature changes to standards/clients since the v0.3.x line
